SMS messaging solutions are used in e-commerce to provide mobile notifications, connect buyers and sellers, and improve relationship management (CRM) programs in a cost-effective, efficient and timely manner.
SMS messaging solutions provide enterprises with high email requirements, a reliable messaging platform that is highly customizable, scalable, and supported by dedicated technical account managers. This makes the safe delivery of mission-critical messages painless.
We offer organizers powerful but easy-to-use SMS marketing services for event and holiday promotions. We also offer SMS marketing solutions for trade fair and exhibition producers as well as festivals.
Extend your relationship with your readers beyond the page through our SMS marketing programs for magazines and publications.
Use the power of SMS marketing to gather supporters, organize meetings, organize political organizations, and empower the community.
Whether you need to make loyal customers quickly aware of last-minute tickets, or whether you want to generate excitement for recently announced programs, Club SMS provide efficient and cost-effective messaging solutions for concert promoters.

original text:
Finn is amazing, and growing so fast! He's a long guy with a tinge of red to his hair, learning more all the time and captivating the hearts of everyone he meets...we swear it!
​ Our fall school has been full of triumph, testimony, discovery of who God really is, and our identity as His Children. There is a profound simplicity in living life with the One who created us just be with Him, and just as He made us: fearfully, wonderfully, and all for love.
Drew did amazing in his first Crossfit competi-tion!
Discipline for the body and the soul.
Since the beginning of YWAM Kona, the founders Loren and Darlene Cunningham have led the missionaries around the property on Thanksgiving morning to thank the Lord for what He has built, and pray for what He will do next on our land. This is the beginning of our Thanksgiving gathering with all the staff and students.
Our school has been challenged and inspired together with many school collaboations this quarter. There are 10 other missions schools currently running, and it is a privilege to rub shoulders from time to time.
This is the last time of worship and prayer before our teams all head around the world for outreach....a powerful time to worship Jesus for who He is and be excited for His purposes for Fiji, Asia, Nepal, the Middle East, and New York!

original text:
The herd mentality is alive and well. Seems like everyone is stampeding to sell their stocks today. Even as the Fed cuts interest rates by three-quarters of a point, far more than had already been priced into the market. And all the Presidential candidates are talking about some sort of economic stimulus plan.
Now the stock market is supposed to be forward looking, and a drop like today's seems to be signaling RECESSION. But is that really in the cards? Sure, there's some pain on the inflation front, mainly due to the steep rise in oil prices last year, and there's some more pain on the home mortgage front, which can reasonably be expected to lower consumer economic activity, to at least some degree. But what people seem to be forgetting is that most of this pain has already been priced into the market, and most companies outside of the mortgage lending business are reporting very reasonable earnings. Today's steep sell-off, with the Dow down over 400 and the NASDAQ over a 100, is not justified by the numbers, but is merely a reflection of the lemming-style mentality that sometimes seems to infect trader's minds.
So what should you do? Don't be another lemming. Buy! Now's the time! Use that old adage of 'buy low, sell high' to your advantage. This opportunity won't last long - just while I've been writing this post, the DOW has risen 200 points and the NASDAQ 50. Sure, it's a little scary going against the tide, but the big prizes never go to chickens.
This entry was posted on January 22, 2008 at 8:36 am	and is filed under Economics. You can follow any responses to this entry through the RSS 2.0 feed. You can leave a response, or trackback from your own site.
